_comment: Template for defining classification themes. Copy this file, rename to themes.json, and fill in your themes.

themes._theme_key_note: Machine identifier. Use snake_case, no spaces. This becomes part of entry IDs.
themes.label: Human-Readable Theme Name
themes._label_note: Short name for display in charts, tables, and reports.
themes.allegation: What this theme alleges, in 1-2 sentences. This frames the analytical lens.
themes._allegation_note: Example: 'Foreign governments use subsidies to create overcapacity in sectors like steel, flooding global markets with below-cost goods.'
themes.domain: Detailed scope description. State explicitly what is IN scope and what is OUT of scope. Include boundary cases.
themes._domain_note: This is the most important field. The classifier uses it to decide borderline cases. Be specific about what counts and what does not. For product-specific themes, explicitly EXCLUDE other products even if mentioned in the same passage.
themes.keywords: comma-separated search terms that indicate relevance to this theme
themes._keywords_note: Used for initial document scanning. Include synonyms, abbreviations, and related terms.

metadata._document_type_note: Common types: policy_report, trade_review, regulatory_catalogue, sanctions_list
